question.text: The Subscription Agreement for Tungabhadra Infrastructure Fund requires investors to make representations about their 'eligible investor' status. Under SEBI AIF Regulations, what is the minimum investment amount for an investor in any AIF?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI AIF Regulations prescribe a minimum investment of INR 1 crore for investors in any AIF (with the exception of employees/directors of the AIF or manager, who have a minimum of INR 25 lakh). This threshold ensures only sophisticated investors with adequate risk appetite participate. INR 25 lakh is the employee/director exception, not the general minimum. **The Trap:** INR 25 lakh is the concessional limit for employees/directors, which candidates may confuse with the general investor minimum. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-A contrasts mutual fund minimums (as low as INR 500 via SIP) with AIF minimums, highlighting the different investor profiles. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The INR 1 crore minimum is per investor per AIF. Ensure your client meets this threshold and has additional liquidity beyond the commitment. An investor who stretches to meet the minimum may not be suitable for AIF investing.
